quoteFirst: “The man under observation brought the young fellows in his company to the station at
quoteLast: The Offenburg express is due to leave at 3.36. It is uncertain which of the party will travel by it; some have hand luggage, and the others none, and it is not yet ascertainable whether the suspected man will accompany them. One of them bought seven tickets for Offenburg, but the party consists of eight, and one among them looks different and has never been seen here before. It is possible that he may be the leader of the expedition, and in the service of the French. How are we to proceed?”
title: Dr. Mabuse, master of mystery
author: Jacques, Norbert, 1880-1954; Clare, Lilian A. (Lilian Ada), 1865- [Translator]

quoteFirst: When it is noon in Paris, it is already 56 minutes after midday at Vienna; 1.25 P.M. at Athens; 2.21 P.M. at Moscow;
quoteLast: at Teheran; 4.42 P.M. at Bombay; and so on.
title: Astronomy for Amateurs
author: Flammarion, Camille, 1842-1925; Welby, Frances A. (Frances Alice), 1862-1947 [Translator]
